subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Inflammation: Friend and Foe
description: A quick win: understand Inflammation: Friend and Foe.

hook.text: Inflammation saved your ancestors from infections. Now chronic inflammation is killing us with heart disease.

buildup.text: Acute inflammation is a targeted response—blood vessels dilate, immune cells rush in, pathogens are destroyed.

discovery.text: The five classic signs—redness, heat, swelling, pain, and loss of function—are all purposeful.

twist.text: Chronic low-grade inflammation from obesity and stress drives heart disease, cancer, and Alzheimer's.

climax.text: Anti-inflammatory diets, exercise, and stress reduction combat the chronic form without blocking the acute.

punchline.text: Acute heals. Chronic kills. Same mechanism, different timeline.

quiz.question: What is the difference between acute and chronic inflammation?
options: Acute is a short targeted healing response; chronic is ongoing and drives disease
They are the same process at different body locations
Chronic is always caused by infection
